programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will former mayor Tracey take the plunge with toilet roll salesman Stuart? And can burlesque dancer Double D and professional singer Lee come together and hit all the right notes?

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Susie Dent and Rachel Riley host the battle of numbers, letters and laughs. Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang play Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. With David O'Doherty.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Jodie wants to start a small horse livery business. She also explores how to start up a kids' club on the family's smallholding.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: In Miami, Charmaine is running a multimillion-pound business from her bedroom while homeschooling her daughter. Outside Orlando, Gary and Maria are expecting more grandchildren.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, can the Rodriguezes find the wow-factor for Darren and Tanya's half a million euros? On the Costa del Sol, the Hamiltons help David and Jackie upgrade their place.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah and Adam from Sussex are calling upon Jean Johansson to help them find a holiday home in the beautiful coastal town of Mojacar. With a budget of £85,000 Jean has five great options.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Samantha and Sam are looking for their dream holiday home in Oliva, Spain, that will work for the whole family. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £85k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mark has a lifelong love of Italy, and having recently retired, he's set his sights on a bolthole in Puglia. Can Laura Hamilton find him a perfect place for his £80k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Half-built walls and a ticking clock haunt Sophie and Ray at Château des Fouilloux. A massive water leak forces Stephanie into a full kitchen rebuild at Château de Lalande.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joe and Kirsty want to swap their Hampshire house for a perfect forever home in the warmer climes of inland Valencia, Spain. Can Jean Johansson find them their dream place for their £280k?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This week's competition is in Windsor where the first to host is estate agent, Vikki, who's hoping to bag the £1000 prize by hosting a ladies day themed dinner party.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second night in Windsor, and radio DJ Mary is looking to impress her guests with an evening of traditional Irish dishes like steak with champ and an Eton Mess with an Irish twist.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The next host in Windsor is outspoken entrepreneur Mohinder, who after two nights of criticising his fellow guests' culinary efforts and personal tastes, needs to pull out all the stops.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four of the competition in Windsor and the turn of retired sales manager and football fan Angie, who's hoping her night of 60s inspired fun will impress her fellow guests.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night in Windsor, and car salesman Rob is about to unleash the mother of all surprises, his enormous country mansion complete with swimming pool and Ferrari.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: When it comes to dating etiquette, Breeze the American bulldog cross leaves a lot to be desired when he meets 31-year-old Charlotte. Will she still fall for him though?

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Having moved from Cape Town to Cardiff, Tish and Ramone's new home is within touching distance... and so is a ceiling. In Newport, will Gemma and Ellis fall for the same house?

programme.title: The Equalizer 2
programme.desc: Robert McCall's mysterious past cuts especially close to home when thugs kill Susan Plummer, his best friend and former colleague. Now out for revenge, McCall must take on a crew of highly trained assassins who will stop at nothing to destroy him.

programme.title: Glass
programme.desc: David Dunn looks to deliver vigilante justice to the Beast -- the madman who has superhuman strength and 23 personalities. Their epic showdown leads them to the mysterious Elijah Price, the criminal mastermind who holds critical secrets for both men.

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: On the American Pacific coast, a female fraudster and leading local pagan feigns serious illness to steal over $500,000 from her victims. And then the FBI gets involved.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host the comedy quiz mash-up. Bob Mortimer and Tom Allen take on Richard Ayoade and Harriet Dyer. With Ian Smith and Joe Wilkinson.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Violent assaults against police have leapt up by a third in just 4 years - meaning that 30 officers are seriously injured in the line of duty every day.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The skills of the interceptors are put to the test as they go in hot pursuit of reckless youths. Jacko is on the hunt for a wanted teenager, and the interceptors are hot on the heels of a stolen silver Micra.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: After a high speed pursuit, interceptor Liam Sewell hunts down a wanted teenager. Meanwhile, Jacko plays a game of cat-and-mouse with a youth who flees on foot.

programme.title: Rough Night in Jericho
programme.desc: Western about a corrupt town boss who meets his match when he takes on the female owner of a stagecoach line. He is intent on taking over the line, but the woman turns out to be a tough opponent and forces a showdown.

programme.title: Ride Clear of Diablo
programme.desc: A railway surveyor travels to Santiago in the hope to avenge the murder of his father and brother. Under the protection of a deputy's badge, he goes after the famous gunslinger he believes is responsible.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Ride Clear of Diablo

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A seven car motorway pile-up leaves a local council's waste truck in trouble. Fluff does his best to shift one of the heaviest loads his vehicle can lift. Meanwhile, Fluff's girlfriend Marta is called to a stranded truck in a quarry.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Craig responds to a report of an incident involving a car on fire with the driver trapped inside. In Derby, as people make their way home from a boozy night out, Alex is on the lookout for drink drivers chancing their luck.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A petrol thief runs out of gas, the helicopter follows a car thief to the fair and a drunk driver does not have to travel far to be patched up when he crashes into a surgery.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: There is a hair-raising pursuit through the market town of Ripley which sees a driver mounting pavements, hitting cars and even colliding with an ambulance. An Interceptor has a narrow escape when an out of control driver crashes into his car. A drunk driver goes for the world record in failed breathalyser tests and the helicopter is called in when a Land Rover driver decides to take a pursuit off-road.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Matt Turner chases down a drink driver who's caused a collision on the morning school run, and on the M6 a broken down BMW stinks of cannabis.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Darrell and Lisa head into Alberta and onto a trail nicknamed The Death Road. Art faces off with the ice crossing where he nearly lost his life last season, and Todd takes rookie Steph Custance under his wing as she embarks on her first journey.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully investigate the possible return of an executed prisoner who vowed to avenge his death.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The Correctional Institution for Women is the oldest and largest women's prison in the Philippines, where the prisoners include petty criminals, drug dealers, murders and gang members.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Way out in Central Australia, outback legend Steve Grahame is hot and bothered 450 kilometres from Alice Springs.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police interceptors tackle a difficult night shift. Ortis is with Spike as they pursue a fleeing car that drives into a dead end, while Rick is with dog handler Ian as they're called to back up the firearms squad.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors take on youngsters who cause carnage on our roads and put themselves and others in danger. Spike and dog handler Ian pursue a 20-year-old drunk driver into a traveller's site. Ben goes head-to-head with a speeding boy racer.

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly
programme.desc: A cattle drover, en route to selling his herd to the Yankees, is kidnapped by a band of ruthless Confederate troopers who plan to drive the herd into their own territory. The situation is inflamed when the cattleman helps the rebel leader's lover to flee from him.

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly

programme.title: Lawman
programme.desc: Frontier tale about an impassive, out-of-town marshal's encounter with the hostile community of Sabbath. When he chases a group of wanted men into the town, the lawman finds that the local sheriff and much of the populace are in the pay of a criminal rancher, and he soon finds himself confronting difficult opposition as he tries to carry out his business.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A burnt out van abandoned on an isolated towpath presents a serious challenge for Steve and Marta, but the solution comes in the form of a bespoke, bright yellow rescue truck. A lorry loaded with tarmac breaks down spurring a race against time.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Drink drivers are caught in the act as they try to beat the system.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Eurocopter gets down and dirty in a lengthy manhunt, a vanload of villains lose control in a high speed pursuit and a drunk driver crushes his Corsa like it is a tin can. Elsewhere, a speed freak thinks he can outrun the law, there is aggravation and abuse on Derbyshire's mean streets and the interceptors facing off against an eight legged enemy.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A horsebox carries an illegal load, one of the team's top cars becomes a victim of a drink-driver and a road user gets a lesson in how to transport children safely.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A van is found to contain a high value counterfeit cargo, a crash leaves a female passenger trapped in a car, and a tractor is used to pursue a suspect.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: A police helicopter is sent to the scene after a car stolen in Gloucester is crashed by a pair of fleeing youths. Meanwhile, a night worker in a petrol station is attacked by a customer.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: When caught with an open container in Florida, a woman lies about her age. Officers ask a group of spring breakers to fill in a huge hole they dug on the beach. A man dressed like Rocky is stopped.

programme.title: Cops
programme.sub-title: Spring Break: Bombed at the Beach
programme.desc: Test kits for illicit substances are handed out to beachgoers. K-9 'Delta' conducts bomb detection drills on the sand. A man asks Deputies to pose for a picture with his summons for underage drinking.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: When a lonely woman on a blind date is brutally murdered, Mulder links her case to earlier disappearances of women looking for love in the wrong places. Scully's analysis reveals strange discrepancies in the victim's weight and body composition - somehow the killer removed all the fat from the corpse - and he is still out there looking for another victim.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Zenica prison is the toughest prison in Bosnia and Herzegovina. Discipline and fitness are essentials that the prison administration regularly reviews.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: As John's retirement looms, he shares one last heavy haul with his son Justin. Meanwhile, Sam competes with traffic to pick up a load of racing livestock.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A coach that has driven off the road into a hedgerow needs to be rescued before rush hour traffic gets disrupted. A dustbin lorry has gotten stuck in the mud on a narrow country road.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In a muddy Leicestershire field, recovery operator Simon is confronted with not one, but two trucks in need of rescuing. Lantern recovery operator Merrie is on her way to urgently assist a stricken unit and trailer that's broken down.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors are after a man wanted on suspicion of making threats to kill, who could be armed, and Coups and police dog Rambo are on the lookout for a suspected shoplifter.

programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.desc: Ant and Mark's dogged pursuit of a stolen moped takes them off their patch into the estates of Sheffield.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The officers intercept a stolen car, foil a cannabis grower, engage in a series of drugs raids and calm a drunken sailor.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers intervene in a public case of spousal abuse, chase after a racing 4x4 and try to break a county-wide car theft operation.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The officers face a 40-mile dash to prevent a potential suicide, one of the Evos comes a cropper on the road and a thief is caught with a haul of stolen clothes in his van.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: Anti-fraud teams expose an £8 million crash-for-cash scam, while a woman claiming she tripped on a loose paving stone with her toddler is caught out by CCTV. (S2 Ep14)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jo presses her father to release Tane now that there's evidence that he is innocent.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf and Brax are fishing. Alf questions his distracted mood, and Brax opens up about his dilemma - whether to stay in the Bay, raise his son here, or return to WA.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Abbey leads the team through a flood of cases in Barnsley, including a patient with cellulitis, an injury after being thrown from a horse, and a toddler with a head wound.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on the night shift as experienced by A&E workers, with exclusive access to Hull Royal Infirmary. The night-time challenges facing NHS staff are captured in their raw form.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Following specialist emergency response teams on their most extreme medical call outs, embedded with the Critical Care and Air Ambulance Response cars.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.desc: Dr Wyne has some advice for Elvira, a Romanian woman, who has brought a cocktail of drugs from her home country to 'treat' her many conditions.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: While there's a shortage of space on specialist wards, a man is blue lit into casualty after a fall, and an ambulance rushes in Betty, 75, who has been vomiting blood.

programme.desc: Professional bass player Caleb has a lump on his forehead that he wants Dr Dev to remove as it's giving him a headache. It's gunk galore as Dr Emma operates on a chest cyst, while Dr Adil debates whether he can remove a large keloid.

programme.desc: At Vauxhall station, one the capital's fare-dodging ultra-hot spots, a team of six revenue protection officers set a trap to block rush hour fare dodgers.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dave is on the lookout for a car driving dangerously, when suddenly it pulls out in front of him. John's 100mph blue-light run panics an unrelated driver into a crash. Veteran cop Jason's `copper's instinct' leads him to suspect a motorcyclist.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Violent assaults against police have leapt up by a third in just 4 years - meaning that 30 officers are seriously injured in the line of duty every day.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Craig responds to a report of an incident involving a car on fire with the driver trapped inside. In Derby, as people make their way home from a boozy night out, Alex is on the lookout for drink drivers chancing their luck.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors catch a gang of car thieves red-handed. Elsewhere, officers clamp down on car crime at Essex's biggest shopping centre and help get the M25 moving after an accident.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A massive manhunt ensues after three suspects flee onto a farm, the team raids a drugs den and officers stumble across a suspicious-looking postman.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A knife-wielding man ruins New Year celebrations and the interceptors race to capture a gang of fugitives. Elsewhere, snow causes chaos on the roads and Grant becomes furious with the guardian of a child.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A serial fraudster with expensive tastes ends up behind bars, while a damaged sofa claim and a prisoner's injury story fall apart when cameras and insurers reveal the truth. (S2 Ep15)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf and Brax are fishing. Alf questions his distracted mood, and Brax opens up about his dilemma - whether to stay in the Bay, raise his son here, or return to WA.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi fantasises to Mackenzie about what the early pregnancy kicks could mean for the baby's future - maybe they're about to raise a future Olympian.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Jane faces staffing shortages as emergencies arrive, including Harry with sepsis, Ian with an eye injury and Isaac with a stone in his ear.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: A young man arrives in excruciating pain from what may be a dangerous pulmonary embolism, staff rush to help a 66-year-old patient in cardiac arrest, and a man is flown in after being gored by a tusked boar.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Lifting the lid on the night shift as experienced by A&E workers, with exclusive access to Hull Royal Infirmary. The night-time challenges facing NHS staff are captured in their raw form.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Leo faces a life-threatening crisis: his liver is failing and he needs an urgent transplant. Daniel needs surgery for a tumour that could make its way to his lungs.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Accused: Guilty or Innocent?
programme.desc: In 2021, Taio Cruz was accused of breaking into a house and assaulting the occupants. Having taken a plea deal, the 24-year-old could be sent to jail. Will she show remorse and take responsibility for her actions or is she playing the system?

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.desc: Ben travels to a remote island in Australia's Great Barrier Reef to live with former Sydney businessman David Glasheen. Since losing a fortune in the 1987 stock market crash, which also cost him his marriage, David has led a Robinson Crusoe-like existence. Ben learns how to pick oysters, brew beer, trade fish and dance with the locals, finding out how it feels to truly live alone.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben travels north to Alaska to live with Bretwood Higman, Erin McCittrick and their two children. Bretwood and Erin hiked 4,000 miles to get here, and the family now lives for half the year in a Mongolian-style yurt on the mountainside, with the other half spent hiking in the wilderness. While staying with them, Ben learns how to chop trees and protect himself from grizzly bears.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Gift Experience Voucher Left Me £200 Out of Pocket
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin ask whether gift vouchers for short breaks and experiences really add up. One man's weekend away would have cost him hundreds of pounds more than if he'd booked direct. And the motorist who only discovered his insurer had stopped sending documents through the post when he was pulled over by the police for driving without insurance.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she's in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

And the winner of this year's Best Balcony and Container Garden is revealed.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Paid For a Smartphone but Received a Lump of Clay
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin come to the aid of people stuck with paying a monthly contract for expensive smartphones that were never delivered, and their phone companies don't seem to want to help.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It's a labour of love for two brothers, who create a wonderful home for their sister in Chesterton, while Martin is puzzled by a door going to nowhere in Plymouth and Dion follows a speedy flat renovation in Wales.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: This show will have animal lovers' tails wagging as our teams scour the country for dogs, birds, fish, pigs and even spiders. Christina counts down the most profitable pet-related buys.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Celebrating a fantastic week at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026, Nicki Chapman and Angellica Bell are joined by Dame Mary Berry to reveal which garden has won the BBC RHS People's Choice Award. Florist to the stars Hamish Powell shows you how to create some fabulous floral displays, and we answer more of your questions in the Chelsea Garden Clinic.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having met online and fallen in love just over a year ago, today's couple are calling on the help of Nicki Chapman to find them their very first home together in the East Sussex countryside. To better understand what living in this county is like, Nicki meets up with one of its residents, none other than fellow Escape to the Country presenter Alistair Appleton. Alistair welcomes Nicki to his beautiful, off-grid, 200-year-old former coastguard's cottage, which he calls home.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: North East - Victorian Stage Machinery Gets an Update
programme.desc: The team restore some Victorian stage machinery, Suzie hears a story of injustice from apartheid South Africa, and wood lover Will gets to grips with the delicate art of marquetry.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts as a team of celebrity comedians, featuring Susan Calman, John Thomson, Jon Culshaw and Shazia Mirza, takes on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth and Adam Frost are joined by special guest Dame Mary Berry, who is on a mission to discover more about growing peonies, one of her favourite flowers. Arit Anderson reveals the winner of Best Houseplant Studio, while Adam Frost and JJ Chalmers uncover all the latest trends to emerge from the show this year.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox and miniaturist Lee bring Silverwood Colliery back to life in miniature for ex-miner Reg and miner's son Neil. From the iconic winding wheel to the miners' banner, every detail recalls the hardships, camaraderie and unforgettable stories of the 1984 strike. Can the model capture the spirit of a community that endured so much?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week is coming to an end, but not before a few more cooks have to say goodbye. Ten contestants remain, and there is only room in the semi-finals for eight.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts, with guest panellists Phil Wang and Judi Love joining team captains Ian and Paul as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: There's trouble brewing in Finglas as Granddad drops a bombshell - he's planning to leave! Agnes, of course, is having none of it.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Having met online and fallen in love just over a year ago, today's couple are calling on the help of Nicki Chapman to find them their very first home together in the East Sussex countryside. To better understand what living in this county is like, Nicki meets up with one of its residents, none other than fellow Escape to the Country presenter Alistair Appleton. Alistair welcomes Nicki to his beautiful, off-grid, 200-year-old former coastguard's cottage, which he calls home.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: North East - Victorian Stage Machinery Gets an Update
programme.desc: The team restore some Victorian stage machinery, Suzie hears a story of injustice from apartheid South Africa, and wood lover Will gets to grips with the delicate art of marquetry.

programme.title: The Marvellous Miniature Workshop
programme.desc: Sara Cox and miniaturist Lee bring Silverwood Colliery back to life in miniature for ex-miner Reg and miner's son Neil. From the iconic winding wheel to the miners' banner, every detail recalls the hardships, camaraderie and unforgettable stories of the 1984 strike. Can the model capture the spirit of a community that endured so much?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Sarah the Lumberjill is the fourth generation of her family to 'take up the saw', working across the forests of the Central Highlands of Scotland, where her family have worked for 150 years. After some of the worst storms to hit Scotland in a lifetime, Sarah, her dad Ralph and brother Ralphy are busier than ever - though with Ralph's health in decline, Sarah has a lot to learn before taking over one day.

programme.title: Back from the Brink
programme.desc: From dolphins in Scotland to bats in Slovenia, Libby Penman showcases European nature stories that celebrate the incredible work of people fighting to save Europe's wildlife.

programme.title: Pitch Invasion: How the Scottish and Irish Changed Football
programme.desc: Manchester United is one of the most successful football clubs in England, but its worldwide success was built on the shoulders of footballing geniuses from both Ireland and Scotland.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: There's a big wedding to organise so the Craiglang pals all rally round to sort a venue, flowers, food and even a band. With such a lot to arrange, will it all go without a hitch? Jack and Victor have a chance encounter with an old pal while enjoying a relaxing sit on a park bench.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2008. After a breakdown, Niall learns that Ruben has returned. Obsession takes hold, and a charged confrontation forces the brothers to face their fractured past.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: After an emotional funeral for his dad, Colin and Cathy have the neighbours round but understandably Colin is not his usual upbeat self. Christine was not impressed with the service or the priest and, as usual, is not backward about coming forward with her opinions on the whole affair. And as Beth, Eric and Ian try to make their excuses to leave early, Cathy manages to foil their escape by inviting more of the neighbours in.

programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores the physics of the size of life through some close encounters with Australia's wildlife. programme.desc: Fascinating finds at St George's Hall in Liverpool include a signed copy of the Beatles' first single, a valuable picture frame, a candelabrum from Hitler's last retreat in Berlin, and a tie-pin that once belonged to Lord Nelson.

programme.title: Flog It!

programme.title: Tiny Tunes

programme.title: I Can Grow It

Murphy has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphy to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Vegesaurs

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: The Days of the Week Badge
programme.desc: The Squirrels are excited to learn that Mr Wobbly, the jelly juggler, is coming to town, and they can't wait to see him. When they become impatient, Duggee explains to them that the week will fly by and that there's lots to be done in that time.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.sub-title: Peekaboo Wall and Treasure Island Walk

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Kyra and Khloe have some garden fun planned. First, they visit a garden centre to buy some new plants, before heading back home to plant them. It's hard work, but Kyra is sure her flowers will grow to be nice and big.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Elsa is using salt fish to make a fishcake recipe she has learnt from her father. She starts by mashing potatoes with butter before adding lots of other tasty ingredients such as chives. Not everyone likes the taste of chives before they are cooked but hopefully they will enjoy them in the finished recipe. When her friends arrive, Elsa greets them with a special pineapple drink served in pineapple-shaped cups.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.sub-title: Day and Night and Solar Panel
programme.desc: Maddie explains how the sun doesn't move when we go from day to night, but how it's the earth turning on its axis that makes the sun appear in the morning and go away again at night. She uses a special time-lapse camera at sunset to show the earth turning away from the sun when it is `going down' at night time. Next, Maddie heads to a solar panel farm.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: It's the Woofton Wolves' last football match of the season, and Rayane is in danger of missing the second half. Hearing dog Diesel helps Rayane at his team's last football match of the season.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Cheetah and Warthog
programme.desc: To meet some beautiful cheetahs, Aneeshwar and Lizzie lay down herbs and spices on logs in their enclosure. The two brothers love to find things with a strong odour. Aneeshwar and Lizzie see how the cheetahs use their sense of smell to achieve it.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle isn't allowed at school, so he's being looked after by Connie, the Brooklyn-Bell's neighbour, at her house. Connie has dressed as a dog and made a huge, doggy maze game from cushions and suitcases for her dog, Jelly, to race through with Waffle. The first to reach a squeaky pig toy wins it. Bad dog Jelly refuses to go through the maze and shortcuts by leaping over it instead, beating Waffle to the prize.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba have fun learning to hunt, fly silently and improve their night vision with the owls. Baba is so impressed that he decides he really wants to be an owl too.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: The potatoes celebrate the four seasons and sing a folksy song about the exciting things that come with each one.

programme.title: Yukee

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie and Phillip really want their toy train to travel all the way from Phillipsburg to Maddiesville, but getting everyone to agree on exactly how is easier said than done.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels can't figure out if fish have noses, Duggee shows them his library. But when the Squirrels realise no-one is visiting it, they have an idea. A mobile library. The Squirrels set off with Duggee. Along the way, they help Tino find his creativity, King Tiger figure out his favourite ice cream flavour and Worm find her love of fantasy. The Squirrels learn just how helpful and informative a library can be.

programme.desc: Three opens a stampoline park, where her friends have splatty fun making inky prints of all the shapes they can make.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every `Alphablock' has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. Young Y wants to know why he can't sing with the vowels, but anything's possible if you try.

programme.title: Teletubbies

They find out that they are amazing hunters who help nature stay in balance and that lots of owls living in an area can be a sign that the habitat is healthy. On the way, they find out all about how owls' amazing hearing and eyesight can help them hunt and spot an owl out looking for its prey!

programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Can't Flirt

programme.sub-title: The One With the Ride Along

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Gary confides in Monica that he intends to ask Phoebe to move in with him, Monica immediately breaks her promise to him and tells Phoebe, who worries that it is too early in the relationship. programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: They'll be travelling from their home in Essex all the way to the Amalfi Coast in Italy, but the first stop on their road trip is the Netherlands, where they try out one of the country's oldest sports, canal jumping. The action continues when the pair don lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is literally pushed to the edge as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel, and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains.

programme.title: Broad City
programme.desc: A runaway teen disrupts the girls' celebration of Ilana's 23rd birthday by leading them on a chase through St Mark's Place.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The off-colour adventures of a group of foul-mouthed elementary school boys in South Park, Colorado. The students, including, Eric Cartman, Stan Marsh, Kyle Broflovski and Kenny McCormick target every conceivable group in outrageous ways.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: `Doctor Who' star Bradley Walsh bravely hands over his phone to play Send To All, pop sensation Kylie Minogue helps Michael surprise members of the audience before performing a beautiful ballad with Jack Savoretti, and a pilot receives an aeroplane-themed surprise to become the latest Unexpected Star of the Show.

programme.title: The Daily Show
programme.desc: 'The Daily Show' is back with a new host but the same irreverent take on the fools, fibbers and various other f-words in today's headlines.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel's new boss and colleague both smoke. Meanwhile, Ross's son and Joey audition for the same commercial, but only one of them can get the part.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Can't Flirt
programme.desc: Ross really likes the pretty pizza-delivery woman, but his flirting technique is awful. Meanwhile, a nasty surprise awaits Joey when he tells everyone about his big television appearance.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys disrespect strangers at the food court. Later, they pose as tailors and ask strangers to settle ridiculous debates. The punishment involves a cake decorating contest.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert discovers Pat smoking and she pleads with him not to tell.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Events take a dramatic twist in the last-ever episode. Ray fears getting his adenoids removed, but Debra convinces him to go through with the surgery.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray Barone's life seems perfect: he's a successful sportswriter living with his wife Debra and three kids, but trouble comes in paradise in the shape of his interfering mother, grumpy father and resentful brother. In this opening episode of the series, Debra wants a peaceful birthday with Ray, so he tells his parents and brother that there's no party: a lie with embarrassing consequences.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra is angered when Ray's problem with showing affection for her becomes a family matter.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray hopes the family-themed eulogy he gives for his Uncle Gus will extinguish the sparks of a festering family feud.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert administers IQ tests to Ray and Debra and then tests their reactions to false results.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she can't bring herself to do it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk
programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebe's husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross' new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant
programme.desc: Monica gets promoted and invites the friends out for dinner to celebrate, but problems arise when the difference in their salaries becomes apparent and the group splits in two. Meanwhile, when Chandler sets a woman up to get a date with her, a case of mistaken identities means that he discovers what she really thought of him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachel's negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds out that her picture of her father, whom she has never known, is just a generic photograph that comes with the picture frame. Elsewhere, after Ross makes Monica's heater malfunction, her Christmas party guests swelter in the middle of winter.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey gets a great acting opportunity on `Days of our Lives', Rachel dates a guy called Russ who is amazingly similar to Ross, and Monica renews her relationship with `Fun' Bobby, but finds out Bobby is only fun when he is drinking.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Lesbian Wedding
programme.desc: Rachel's free-spirited mother visits and reveals that she wants to end her marriage and experiment sexually. Meanwhile, Carol and Susan prepare for their wedding, with some help from an uncomfortable Ross.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Joey starts dating a fan who is gorgeous, but soon discovers that she's also quite mad. Meanwhile, Ross is reminded how much he misses Marcel his old pet monkey, and travels to San Diego Zoo to find out what has happened to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After the Super Bowl
programme.desc: Ross finally spends some time with Marcel after many false starts, and Chandler is delighted to get a date with the sexy Susie, who he hasn't seen since fourth grade. However, she gets her revenge for youthful humiliation by leaving him in the loo, naked but for a pair of her knickers.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Prom Video
programme.desc: When Monica's parents clear out her old room to install a gym, they unearth an old video of her and Rachel's first prom night. It's obvious from this that Ross is in love with Rachel and, having told him it will never happen, Rachel changes her mind. Elsewhere, Joey gives Chandler a hideous bracelet as a token of his friendship and is mortally offended to hear Chandler saying how terrible it is.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross and Rachel ... You Know
programme.desc: Among the guests at Monica's party is a handsome and sophisticated doctor, but the only problem is that he's old enough to be her father. Meanwhile, Joey buys a big screen TV and two reclining leather armchairs with the money from his `Days of Our Lives' contract.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Moves Out
programme.desc: Everyone at Monica's dad's birthday party finds it amusing that their middle-aged friend, eye-doctor Richard Burke, is dating a woman in her 20s - until Monica's parents learn that it is their daughter. Meanwhile, Chandler and Joey row over a dirty spoon. Is the apartment still big enough for both of them?

programme.title: See No Evil, Hear No Evil
programme.desc: Lighthearted adventures of a pair of news vendors in New York - one deaf, one blind - who go on the run when they are blamed for the murder of a customer. Each has seen - or heard - just a whiff of a clue, and a frantic chase ensues as the duo struggle to clear their names.

programme.title: Jimmy Carr's Am I the A******?
programme.sub-title: Hotel Hell and Kitchen Sink Drama
programme.desc: ...A******? Jimmy Carr, GK Barry & Jamali Maddix are back with more potential A******s for your consideration. Meet a man who dumped his mum on holiday and someone with a rather dubious ick.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Actress Emily Bett Rickards and comic Gary Anthony Williams join the regulars for skits, including Scenes from a Hat.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Host Aisha Tyler joins comic regulars along with special guest comic Keegan-Michael Key as they perform improvised sketches prompted by ideas from the audience.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Host Aisha Tyler and the cast are joined by actor Keegan-Michael Key to help with the latest improv comedy skits.

programme.title: South Park: The 25th Anniversary Concert

programme.title: Modern Horror Stories
programme.desc: We're looking into how to deal with being ghosted; Mediums for the ghosted.

programme.desc: Russell performs his usual gleefully juvenile brand of social commentary and discusses some of the joy to be found in everyday experiences. Recorded in front of his home crowd in Bristol.

programme.title: The Daily Show

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ride Along
programme.desc: The gang persuade Phoebe's policeman boyfriend to let them ride in his police car. The friends enjoy the experience, until a gunshot rings out. Elsewhere, Rachel overhears a telephone message for Ross from his second ex-wife.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: After Gary confides in Monica that he intends to ask Phoebe to move in with him, Monica immediately breaks her promise to him and tells Phoebe, who worries that it is too early in the relationship. programme.desc: Ray begs his parents to be nice when his in-laws come to town for a visit.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Moves In
programme.desc: With Joey in his new apartment, Chandler gets a troublesome new flatmate, Eddie. Meanwhile, Phoebe makes a video for `Smelly Cat', and Ross and Monica find it nearly impossible to live together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Dr Ramoray Dies
programme.desc: Joey's remarks to a fan magazine could cause him to lose his job playing Dr Ramoray on a daytime television drama, and Chandler's new flatmate Eddie becomes too annoying to cope with.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Eddie Won't Go
programme.desc: Chandler wants his annoying new roommate to move out, Joey is forced to face some exorbitant bills, and the girls learn from a book about personal empowerment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Old Yeller Dies
programme.desc: Monica's new boyfriend impresses Chandler and Joey, Phoebe finds out what she missed as a child, and Rachel resents the fact that Ross is planning the rest of their lives together.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica is broke and decides to gamble on the stock market, and Chandler and Ross run into a couple of bullies in Central Park. Meanwhile, Rachel and Joey help Phoebe locate her father, but a vicious dog prevents Phoebe from finally meeting him. After accidentally injuring the dog with her car, Phoebe gets to know her father's second wife whom she never knew about, and another long-lost family member.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica decides to hold a surprise birthday for Rachel, but everything goes wrong when Rachel's estranged parents turn up unexpectedly. The party then splits into two factions: a dreary gathering in Monica's apartment, and a college-style drunken party at Chandler and Joey's flat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Chicken Pox
programme.desc: Phoebe's submariner boyfriend visits New York on leave from the Navy. Unfortunately, Phoebe catches chicken pox at baby Ben's first birthday party. Meanwhile, when Monica realises that Richard has no real neuroses, she worries that she is too strange for him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Barry and Mindy's Wedding
programme.desc: When Rachel serves as a bridesmaid at the wedding of her ex-fiancé Barry and her friend Mindy, she relives the nightmare of her own failed wedding day. Meanwhile, Chandler becomes enamoured with a woman he meets on the internet, but is surprised when he finally meets her.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: The Story Continues
programme.desc: Kevin returns to the SECC to perform a record-breaking run of ten sold out nights to over 75,000 ecstatic fans. Recorded on a Saturday night in Glasgow, Kevin is in his element, as this home town gig shows off his no-nonsense approach to comedy.

programme.title: Al Murray Live: Barrel of Fun
programme.desc: Britain's best-known pub philosopher serves up his own special brew of comic bar-room banter in his 2010 show.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha, Wayne, Colin, and Ryan are joined by Heather Anne Campbell to play Hollywood Director, Worlds Worst, Song Styles, Scenes From a Hat, Dubbing, and Doo-Wop. Featuring special guest fitness trainer Jillian Michaels.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Host Aisha Tyler joins comic regulars along with special guests comic Jeff Davis and professional athlete Lolo Jones as they perform improvised sketches prompted by ideas from the audience.

programme.desc: Host Aisha Tyler joins comic regulars along with special guests comic Jeff Davis and actor Adelaide Kane as they perform improvised sketches prompted by ideas from the audience.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. These `Cat Polls' are primed to root out the important issues of the day, like what is Britain's most unwanted wedding gift or who the British public would most like to have round for dinner. Guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse join the regulars.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr delves into the world of opinion polls with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson who are joined by guests Johnny Vegas, Vernon Kay, Paul Chowdhry and Katherine Ryan.

programme.desc: Neil deGrasse Tyson relaxes at home, Keegan endures a job interview, a tailor has a gassy client, the valets discuss `Game of Thrones,' and a new strategy for telemarketing.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and the team are in Blackburn where they come face to face with a tiger, other items of interest include a Doulton facemask which Corrie Jeffrey worries she has been charmed by, and Mark Stevens meets his match when he haggles with a father and son over a gentleman's wristwatch.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and his dealers Michael Hogben, Mark Stevens, Stewart Hofgartner and Corrie Jeffrey are in Blackburn. Items of interest include a Victorian wax seal, a Clarice Cliff jug and an obscure telephone repair kit.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Burton-on-Trent is the location today as a naive marine painting comes through the door - but is the valuation as naive? and there are some saucy postcards that make Jo Brayshaw chuckle.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Skipton is the location today as Cheryl Hakeney gets herself in a spin over an Art Deco bronze figure, other items brought along include a Royal Doulton Toby jug, and a cavalier sword.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Blackburn plays host to David Dickinson and his team of dealers as Mark Stevens goes head to head with a particularly savvy seller over a Georgian sugar caster, Michael Hogben is determined to get his hands on an art deco lamp and a silver necessaire lands on Corrie Jeffrey's table.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.desc: Rugby fan Andy and dental nurse Holly bring a Mexican fiesta to Yorkshire. Mitesh and Jess serve an Indian-inspired meal to spice-averse guests. And, Paul and Mandy dish out a few surprises.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Robbie Williams superfan Trish and Dave, quirky Patrick and Lizzie, and seasoned hosts John and Lynne compete in Stoke, in an edition featuring wartime food and a good old knees-up.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Gloucester rugby lad Josh is desperate to please his demanding bride-to-be, Stacie. They met five years ago in a nightclub and Josh popped the question in 2010. Money is tight, so they are given £12,000 pounds for their big day. However, they have agreed that Josh will arrange the wedding on his own and Stacie won't know a single thing until the big day in three weeks' time.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Paintballing Hen Do
programme.desc: It was love at first sight for car sprayer Cameron and teaching assistant Sarah when they met in their local pub. Cameron is one of the last in the family to marry, and struggles to please everybody as he plans the perfect wedding.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: True Value CEO John Hartmann, a former FBI agent, goes under cover and is shocked by the antics of an employee.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Pittsburgh Mayor Bill Peduto tackles undercover roles of municipal employees including a refuse worker, a carpenter and a self-sufficiency coordinator.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is back in Woodland Park, Colorado, for the conclusion of his attempt to help struggling Italian restaurant Mangia Mangia avoid ruin. Will harassed owner Julie ever be reconciled with her out-of-control head chef?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon's in Redondo Beach, California, to visit Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece duo Faye and Brenda.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Fed up with her DIY disaster after nearly a decade, hardworking single mum Catherine is determined to finally flip the problematic property.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In County Antrim, newlyweds Rebecca and Gareth, Nel and Billy, and Mandy and Jim compete in a week featuring a Mexican fiesta, risky puddings and a vegetarian who doesn't like vegetables.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton delivers a timely message of peace. Joanne McNally invents a new catchphrase. Nick Mohammed and Sophie Willan both struggle with a bottle of water. John Robins gets creative with beans. And Alex Horne feels the thrill of victory.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a new edition of the panel game with Sean Lock and a special guest captain. The teams are joined by celebrity guests. Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr host a new edition of the panel game, with Sean Lock and Freddie Flintoff who take on special guest captain Bill Bailey with Roisin Conaty.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will's mum is away for a weekend with old schoolfriend Fergus. Will is left home alone and the other boys take full advantage of the situation. They hammer the Ocado account, empty the fridge and rifle through Polly's knicker drawer among other things, but despite this there's a chance even Will might have a good time. That is of course until morning.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will decides to organise one last trip for the gang before they go their separate ways. Our four heroes pack the clapped-out Fiat for a camping trip in the countryside. Will draws on his scout training, the same however, cannot be said for Jay, Neil and Simon who aren't prepared for the great outdoors at all.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Sketch deals with Lee's World Cup wobble. Pash sorts Daniel's not-so-flattering memento. Uzzi covers Harry's catty catchphrase and smokes out Chelsea's slip-up.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton delivers a timely message of peace. Joanne McNally invents a new catchphrase. Nick Mohammed and Sophie Willan both struggle with a bottle of water. John Robins gets creative with beans. And Alex Horne feels the thrill of victory.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a new edition of the panel game with Sean Lock and a special guest captain. The teams are joined by celebrity guests. Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Erica at a crossroads about her future, she draws inspiration from Baby Boom and crafts a plan to have it all; Adam remakes the "Uptown Girl" music video, meets Carmen's father and learns to be more of a "downtown man."

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Things are finally working out for Adam, that is until he receives a letter from NYU that could change everything; when Barry hears the JTP have other friends outside their crew, he fears the end of their posse as they know it.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: It's high noon for impulsive groom Steve as he plans a cliff-hanger of a wild west wedding to Leona. Will Steve's nuptials on a bridge prove a swinging success?

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: It's Phil's birthday and he desperately wants the year's must-have gadget. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to find his inner mean machine.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Jay take the reigns when Luke and Manny's hot-tempered basketball coach quits during a game. Meanwhile, Mitchell invites Cameron to meet a prospective employer.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorcycle and bicycle variety.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue and Shaun kick off the competition in Bournemouth with a Mediterranean menu, but one guest proves hard to please. Perfectly preened pair John and Livi add some glamour to proceedings.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasn't taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesn't slow down he's in danger of collapse.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Oakhurst, New Jersey, to help Mike Farber, the overwhelmed owner of Mike and Nellie's restaurant, who lost his way when Nellie, his father and business partner, died. Opened in 1996, the restaurant was a lifelong dream for the father and son. Alone at the helm, and still mourning the loss of his father, Mike has taken his eye off the ball and the restaurant has gone to pot.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: Wedding bells ring for the final pair in this year's experiment, before all five couples jet off to Panama for their honeymoons.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Luke and Manny have their first day of high school; Claire returns to the workforce; Mitch juggles an important meeting and Lily's first day of preschool.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil's job takes up a lot of his time; Luke has his friends over for a poker game; Gloria worries the baby is cursed; Cam hosts a funeral for the cat's imaginary wife.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa fears she is genetically doomed to lose her intelligence.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge invites Ralph Wiggum over for a play-date with Bart. When Bart goes back to the Wiggum household and finds it filled with police equipment and files, he is in a troublemaker's paradise.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting his millionaire half-brother, Homer sets out to design the perfect car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Abe tells Marshall Goldman of the time he was on a World War II battleship and the story is later published in the Springfield Shopper. As a result, his Springfield popularity increases rapidly and he decides to `dump' Homer as his son.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: After making an arrest without much proof, Jake has just 48 hours to collect enough evidence before the perp is released.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake Peralta is overjoyed when his hero, veteran crime reporter Jimmy Brogan, spends some time at the precinct.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay is determined to get the adults' hotel suite upgraded. Claire tries to win back some money.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch struggles to hide his genuine feelings about an unflattering wedding topper Cam's father made. Phil tries to help Gloria sell the apartment she had before Jay but they get sidetracked at a salon. Jay wants to host a nice family evening.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family joins Phil as he travels to Australia to fulfil his mother's wish that he see the land where he was conceived.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil cannot handle the stress when he tells Claire an elaborate lie, Gloria is tasked with organizing this year's family portrait and Jay covertly enters Stella in a dog show.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Dad Who Knew Too Little
programme.desc: Lisa is upset that Homer doesn't know more about her, so he hires a detective to spy on her, but when Homer fails to pay him, the detective frames Lisa for a crime and she has to go on the run.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Inspired by Ned Flanders, Homer turns to prayer in the hope of changing his lot in life. After he is injured coming out of church, he sues for damages and is awarded the church itself.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart discovers the existence of an embarrassing TV commercial he appeared in as a baby, and that Homer spent the money he earned, he sues for emancipation and moves out of the house.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: I'm Spelling as Fast as I Can
programme.desc: Lisa wins a place at the Spellympics National Spelling Bee, but discovers that it's not as clean-cut a competition as she thought, while Homer tours the country on the trail of a limited edition Krustyburger.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart enters his new robot into the Robot Rumble TV show without realising that it's Homer in a costume. Meanwhile, Lisa has cat trouble.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes a neighbourhood hero when he buys a snowplough and clears his neighbours' driveways.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: One couple struggle to deal with a massive bombshell on their honeymoon in Panama. And one bride gets a surprise gift.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Dating guru Anna Williamson sends Coleen Nolan out with a joker. And will there be splinters or sparks when Lucinda Light goes axe throwing with Damian?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: A celebration of Naked Attraction's very best naughty bits, revisiting times when the pickers got it spot on and chose their perfect date, including Gemma and James who found love.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Science technician Ian is 31 but has never been on a date. Can he find a spark with shy nursery nurse Stacie? Glamourous 22-year-old dental nurse Cece is looking for someone who can see that her beauty is more than skin deep.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Dating guru Anna Williamson sends Coleen Nolan out with a joker. And will there be splinters or sparks when Lucinda Light goes axe throwing with Damian?

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: One couple struggle to deal with a massive bombshell on their honeymoon in Panama. And one bride gets a surprise gift.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and some friends organise a protest against the male population of Springfield after they become obsessed with Mixed Martial Arts.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Four wittily woven yarns featuring tipsy terrors, revenge plots, sinister sandwiches and a Sweeney Todd homage. Expect bloodshed and belly laughs.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam takes Beverly to her high school reunion, he tries to end a reality where she winds up with the wrong man; Barry and Joanne make a surprising decision to prove the seriousness of their relationship.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Hoping to receive a car for his birthday, Barry is shocked by what his mother gives him. Elsewhere, Adam asks Pop for advice.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Wight Wedding
programme.desc: Fun-loving groom-to-be Liam wants a wedding day that really celebrates his native Isle of Wight, but his future wife Kandi is hoping for a more conventional day.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire is dubious of Phil's father when he pays a visit with a dog and RV in tow. Meanwhile, Jay and Gloria take Manny to see a horror movie and Cameron fills a temporary void in Dylan's band.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A surprise birthday trip to Hawaii for Jay is marred by the fact that his whole family is tagging along. The chaos begins at the airport with forgotten identification, security breaches and flying phobias.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Newcastle, there's wine snobbery, a fairy-tale feast and a Moroccan menu based solely on one couple's love of hummus.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Austin, Texas, home to struggling restaurant El Greco, a business in danger of imminent collapse. El Greco is owned by mother and son Athina and Jake, and claims to be an authentic Greek restaurant. Much to Gordon's disgust, the duo have secretly recruited `Chef Mike' the microwave to help them out on a daily basis.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon is in Atlanta, Georgia, to visit Michon's, a family-run smokehouse restaurant with a future that's about to go up in smoke because of mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped that one day he would pass his beloved restaurant over to his daughter, Natalie. But when he suffered a collapsed lung, the handover came about much earlier than expected.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: One couple struggle to deal with a massive bombshell on their honeymoon in Panama. And one bride gets a surprise gift.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: When Cam's sister comes to visit, he tries to find a gentle way to break the news of his engagement; Phil and Claire decide to miss one of Luke's games; Gloria is in denial about needing glasses.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The adults struggle to make it to a hot new restaurant in time for the reservation Jay pulled a lot of strings to get.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer gets into deep water when he joins the naval reserve after getting fired from the nuclear plant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer and Mr Burns go on the run to Cuba with a trillion-dollar note that Mr Burns stole from the government many years ago.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Once Upon a Time in Springfield
programme.desc: Krusty's show is losing its female ratings and his executives implement the hire of new female co-star, Princess Penelope. Meanwhile, Mr Burns takes away the free doughnuts from the nuclear power plant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer wins the lottery after skipping a date with Marge. Fearing Marge's rejection, he keeps his winnings a secret while showering his family with anonymous gifts.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Sal's Pizza, one of Jake Peralta's favourite haunts, burns down, the fire marshal assumes that the owner is the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, Jake does everything in his power to prove him wrong.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Amy Santiago hosts a Thanksgiving dinner at her apartment for all of her colleagues at the precinct. However, the celebration risks turning into a holiday disaster when the catering falls flat, and Captain Holt and Jake Peralta are forced to leave.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay, Gloria and Manny challenge one another to try something new.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A series of crises on Mitch and Cam's wedding day put Pepper to the test, Phil gets an eye procedure before the celebration and Claire embraces the role of best woman.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Chaos forces Mitch and Cam to move from one contingency plan to another, but an unexpected turn of events allows the day to move forward.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Cam and Mitch return from their honeymoon and adjust to married life.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When a famous and sultry movie star swoops into Springfield with eyes for none other than Ned Flanders, he becomes the envy of Springfield, and Homer is dying to know if they ever did the `deed'. The movie star eventually tires of Flanders and returns to LA, but Flanders still refuses to tell Homer what he wants to know. Naturally, Homer goes nuts.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons become fed up with the new flight patterns at the Springfield airport, taking a stand they demand action from their congressman.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer enrolls in a `How to Succeed in Being You' course and takes a new attitude to work, where he makes suggestions to improve safety at the power plant. When Burns laughs at Homer's proposals, Homer and Lisa devise a plan to take the plant from him. After being legally outmanoeuvred, Burns signs the plant over to Homer, and Homer becomes the new CEO.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: 'Scuse Me While I Miss the Sky
programme.desc: A British film-maker comes to Bart's class to make a documentary on the typical American school. Bart's celebrity status is plunged into jeopardy when Nelson receives attention from the film-maker for stealing hood ornaments. Moreover, the film-maker leaves Lisa with feelings of inadequacy, causing her to run out and choose a career in astronomy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is delighted with the critical acclaim Marge receives for a novel she writes until he discovers the plot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family struggles to get Maggie to say her first word. Homer says he's glad that Maggie has not spoken yet because all Lisa and Bart do is talk back to him, but after he puts Maggie to bed, she says her first word.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: The honeymoons are coming to an end, and normal life is looming. And that's really quite daunting for some of the less lovey-dovey couples.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: PK Humble needs no encouragement to get his flirt on but gets more than he bargained for. And there's unfiltered feedback from dates old and new.

programme.title: White House Down
programme.desc: While on a tour of the White House with his young daughter, a police officer springs into action to save his child and the president from a heavily armed group of paramilitary invaders.

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: First to see if Naked Attraction can lead them to love is 28-year-old single mum Tracy from Bedfordshire. She's hoping this revealing new way of dating will help her find a man who matches her adventurous side.

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: PK Humble needs no encouragement to get his flirt on but gets more than he bargained for. And there's unfiltered feedback from dates old and new.

programme.desc: Erica's moodiness puts the family roller-skating tradition at risk, while Beverly takes Adam school clothes shopping.

programme.title: The Spoilers
programme.desc: Set in late 19th-century Alaska, the owner of a profitable mine has a fight-and-a-half on his hands to save it from an unscrupulous rival. When the mine-owner falls victim to a scheme engineered by his rival and a corrupt judge, he and his partner team up with a saloon owner to protect their rights.

programme.title: Shadow of a Doubt
programme.desc: Young Charlie is thrilled when her favourite uncle arrives for a visit, but soon begins to suspect that he could be the notorious `Merry Widow Murderer'.

programme.title: Drums Along the Mohawk
programme.desc: John Ford's classic account of a Revolutionary War-era farmer's attempts to build a life in the Mohawk valley.

programme.title: The Day After Tomorrow
programme.desc: Meteorologist Jack warns of terrifying gales and floods, but few listen. By the time Earth's leaders act, millions have been killed by polar ice. Can Jack beat the deep freeze to rescue his stranded son? His son Sam is in New York trapped with a number of others in the library, and knowledge gained from his father helps him keep the group alive.

programme.title: Fury
programme.desc: During World War II, a battle-hardened army sergeant leads the crew of a Sherman tank on a deadly mission behind enemy lines to strike at the heart of Nazi Germany.

programme.title: Glory
programme.desc: Based on the true story of a regiment of black soldiers in the American Civil War who are led by an inexperienced white officer in the fight against the Confederacy. The soldiers and their commander must overcome the racism of fellow Union officers and prove their valour on the battlefield.

programme.title: Franklyn
programme.desc: A masked vigilante detective searches for his nemesis on the streets of Meanwhile City, a monolithic fantasy metropolis ruthlessly governed by faith and religious fervour.
