programme.title: The Mouse That Roared
programme.desc: Classic farce in which the tiny Grand Duchy of Fenwick declares war on the United States with the aim of glorious defeat and a hefty sum in post-war aid. An advance party travels to New York and kidnaps the inventor of the Q-bomb, unfortunately forcing the Americans into immediate surrender.

programme.title: Written on the Wind
programme.desc: Emotional upheavals abound for a rich Texas oil family when their son and his childhood friend fall for the same woman.

programme.title: Hail the Conquering Hero
programme.desc: Preston Sturges' satire about a sickly soldier discharged from duty and mistaken for a war hero when he returns home.

programme.title: The Story of Robin Hood and His Merrie Men
programme.desc: Robin Hood, the dashing rogue, embarks on a legendary campaign against the evil Prince John and the Sheriff of Nottingham.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Apocalypse Now
programme.desc: The definitive examination of the American experience in the Vietnam War, which centres on captain Benjamin Willard who is sent up river to find and kill insane renegade colonel Walter Kurtz.

programme.title: Underwater
programme.desc: Disaster strikes more than six miles below the ocean surface when water crashes through the walls of a drilling station. Led by their captain, the survivors realise that their only hope is to walk across the sea floor to reach the main part of the facility. But they soon find themselves in a fight for their lives when they come under attack from mysterious and deadly creatures that no one has ever seen.

programme.title: Thief
programme.desc: After a stint in prison, a diamond thief has high hopes of going straight, until he is offered a job he can't refuse.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul explores an area previously unknown to him, Norwegian baking. After a helicopter ride to see how Bergen sits within the landscape, he meets a family who bake national treats and creates his version of two of Bergen's favourite bakes.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.sub-title: Making It up Isn't Making Do
programme.desc: Food writer Nigel Slater creates a week's worth of simple yet delicious meals, using everyday ingredients to rustle up sumptuous food that is both inspiring and achievable. Nigel encourages us to experiment in the kitchen and have the confidence to make dishes up as we go along. He also visits local allotment holders and creates a tasty feast from the fruits of their labour.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The remains of the Sunday roast are the starting point for Nigel, as he makes amazing food out of seemingly uninspiring leftovers. It may sound frugal, but Nigel uses his culinary creativity to show why he is one of the nation's favourite foodies. He also visits local allotment holders, and is challenged to cook something on the plot using the freshest of ingredients.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making a spicy food delivery and spilling the hot sauce on the food her family loves. There's her daughter's favourite, Kung Pao chicken, and spicy Sriracha popcorn is a winner with the entire family.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's time for evening meals in Ree's 16-minute frontier fast lane! On the menu are speedy shrimp tacos, pork chops with wine and garlic, zesty lemony green beans, unbeatable pepperoni chicken and crispy grilled cheese sandwiches.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes high-speed ginger shakin' beef, crispy squid and smashed avocado, pork and mash gratin, and almond, orange and polenta cake.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a crazy good pork burger, sticky mango prawns, rustic gnocchi and frozen banoffee cheesecake.

programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Best in Class

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Pastry Week and the bakers put their stamp on a Cornish classic in the signature, go retro in the technical, and make a showstopping classic tart hidden within a cage.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary embraces the British countryside with fresh and honest bucolic cooking inspired by what is grown on farms and in gardens, from a joyous pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus, and how to serve up the most scrumptious roasted vegetables. Mary makes her way through vegetable rows and orchards, ending with her own inspired version of the delicious tarte tatin.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: The cake-loving chef heads into Philadelphia, makes a Fire and Ice cake, and hunts for the notorious pumpple cake, which is baked with sweet pies inside.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: Foodie fun as James samples Butter Cake and meets a man who has brought a taste of Florida to Brooklyn, making authentic Key Lime Pies.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James visits a bakery made famous by `Sex and the City'. Back in the kitchen, James whips up a strawberry gateaux and has some fun with cornflake cookies.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James's cake adventures leads him to find out about two of New York's classic puddings, the New York cheesecake and Baked Alaska.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' British adventure has crossed over the Irish sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by his great pal and famous local chef, Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James settles right in and cooks up a fish stew for the crowds of tourists. Paul goes in search of Irish gold and finds it in the form of rapeseed at the Kane family farm in Limavady.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Paul are having a blast in Northern Ireland and they've reached Paul's home town of Belfast, where he's giving James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city before they head off for a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings. After all his exertion on the golf course, Paul heads just out of town to Carnbrooke Meats.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation
programme.desc: Adam Richman's eating expedition has brought him to the capital city of the Ocean State - Providence, Rhode Island. Adam first heads over to Bob And Timmy's to try their unique grilled pizza.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Nigel shows how combinations of ingredients that grow in the same place at the same time often make the ideal starting point for a dish. There is no need to write down complicated recipes as Nigel's unflustered style makes these feasts easy to remember. Nigel also visits local allotment holders and creates a tasty feast from the fruits of their labour.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: Mouth-watering chocolate cookies and crispy duck with honey and figs prove that a little bit of what you fancy does you good. Then to balance the scales, Nigel makes a couple of dishes full of clean-tasting flavours, with fresh herbs, tomatoes and crunchy greens - but whether saints or sinners, every dish will taste truly divine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree fills the fridge with her kids' favourite foods while they all help out at the Merc. She prepares Paige's quiche, restorative chicken noodle soup and berry yoghurt parfaits.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree tests delicious ideas for kitchen hacks to up her breakfast game. Recipes include shortcut chocolate chip hazelnut pancakes, crazy omelettes in a bag, cheesy wafflemaker hash browns and mango marvel smoothie packs.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a tasty tikka roast chicken, a vibrant carrot and grain salad, a super-speedy crab spaghetti, and Jamie's chocolate orange shortbread.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks a super-fast, stress-free sausage carbonara with a peppery punch. There's also a spicy one-pot Thai red chicken soup; a scrambled egg omelette; and steamed pudding with a speedy, sticky marmalade glaze.

programme.desc: Nigella's in the pink in this edition, using a classic strawberry ice cream to rework the traditional strawberries and cream dish. Other recipes given the Lawson makeover are spiced pinked soup, watermelon, feta and black olive salad, and pink seared lamb with bulgur wheat salad.

programme.title: Forever Summer With Nigella
programme.desc: Nigella travels to Whitstable and makes ricotta hotcakes with strawberries, golden coconut and turmeric-infused fish curry, a white lady cocktail, Vietnamese rice paper rolls and chocolate and passion fruit mousse.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britain's beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the world's industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Weird Britain
programme.desc: Andy embarks on a mission to unearth the Cornish sites of King Arthur, before magnet fishing for Excalibur and visiting the grave of a legendary witch.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet' was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeare's rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Focusing on Roman Britannia, where finds include the thickening mystery of 97 baby skeletons found by the Thames, a newly discovered town in rural Devon that turns history on its head, and a Roman cult figure buried for 1700 years beneath a fort.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in London's Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if it'll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers' cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in London's East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in London's Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barber's in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a derelict 200-year-old listed cottage in Cumbria, another cottage in Cornwall as well as a huge retail building in Wimbledon.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks around terraced projects in Staffordshire and South Wales, while Lucy looks at a small, rundown detached cottage on a large plot with huge potential in Surrey.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy look around a modern detached house, a listed cottage and a terraced house ripe for extending. They all need money spent on them, but at the end of the projects, will there be profits for any of the developers?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a recently married man who with his bride buys a three-bedroom house in Streatham. He jokes that although she is an interior designer he is worried because she has expensive taste.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Barnaby and Jones uncover a series of seething grudges within the village.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britain's best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britain's best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass hatches a plan to poach on a grand scale from Ashfordly Hall while Nick finds himself being harassed by Lord Ashfordly's sister.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police are sent to investigate a theft from a fur factory and Greengrass thinks he has found gold with his metal detector.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DCI Vera Stanhope nets two cases when an ex-prisoner's body is discovered on the banks of the Tyne, and must find out if the former inmate been served cold justice, or whether his death runs deeper.

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Alphy is stunned to discover that a woman he knows has gone missing; when her friend is later brutally murdered, Alphy and Geordie race to find the answers.

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie and Larry are called out to investigate the mysterious death of a reclusive man on the streets of Cambridge; as they investigate the apparent suicide, Alphy and Geordie unravel a series of clues that leads them to a shocking revelation.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: When Rachel bumps into an ex-juror, she is shocked to discover that the woman had an affair with Nick in the middle of a trial - and a desperately worried Nick does everything in his power to persuade Rachel to keep the knowledge to herself. After a current investigation throws up similarities to Veronica's murder, Janet tells Geoff Hastings that they could be one step closer to bringing his sister's killer to justice.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: As Rachel is walking home, she is narrowly missed by a car which then smashes into the side of the road. The next day, she learns that the crash is linked to the disturbing information she found out about Nick's affair with a juror. Was Rachel targeted - and could Nick have had something to do with it? The latest case for the team is that of a teenager shot dead at home, a death witnessed by his best friend.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The new clippie is outraged that the female clippies, unlike male clippies, have to pay to use the public conveniences, so she lobbies the management for an allowance to cover the cost.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry and Simon make money out of old junk. The boys head to Middlesex and Surrey, where Simon repurposes a 50s hairdryer and updates an old school desk. Meanwhile Henry's restoration of a child's motorbike goes down well with his son.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are on the South Coast. Henry gets to work an aircraft fuel tank while Simon gets obsessed with an iconic 60s speedboat.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This competition kicks off in Runswick Bay, Yorkshire and The Cliffemount Hotel, with general manager Garry Healey and housekeeper Zara Doherty. They hope the views be enough to wow the guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Next up is the Quayside Hotel in Boston, Lincolnshire, run by Alistair and partner Lana, where most of the guests are bowled over by the bling, but Claire feels stuck in a neon nightmare.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to Hollington Park Glamping near Newbury, run by couple Claire and Adam. Daniele and Chantal are happy glampers, but the other guests are shocked by their surroundings.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Swan House in Kelvedon, Essex, where owner Daniele is joined by sister Chantal. On arrival, the traditional décor impresses, but Lana is left with wardrobe woes.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the last day of the competition and the bed and breakfast owners are meeting for the last time. After some scathing scores for cleanliness, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first day of the competition in Weston-super-Mare, Zumba teacher Amanda is determined to win her guests over with her locally sourced Somerset menu, including pork in cider. Her night kicks off with some friendly banter until young upstart Harry tries to guess everyone's ages.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, rugby-mad sports centre manager Harry hopes to impress his guests with a menu of Hungarian classics, from beef in hunter's sauce to bird's milk with meringue dumplings. But drama teacher Nancy is out for revenge.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, drama teacher and music lover Nancy throws an Austrian-themed party to reflect her love of the country and its music. Her menu includes Austrian specialities including Wiener schnitzel and venison stew. Her night starts with hilarity when rugby player Harry thinks the clothes he found on the house tour are fancy dress costumes.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Weston-super-Mare, farm estate manager Chris hosts an evening based on his love of all things African, with savoury donuts, deep fried beef and exotic dessert. Chris's donuts are a new taste experience for most of his guests, but go down well.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Businesswoman Tracy hosts the final dinner party in Weston-super-Mare. Tracy has designed her evening around her love of dance, from the tango to the cha cha cha. She's asked her guests to wear black tie and ballgowns, and for entertainment they'll get a dance lesson from Tracy.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the Hillcroft in Torquay, where husband and wife team Martyn and Elaine Flint hope their lovingly decorated adults-only B&B will prove a hit with their guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit sees Sam and Henry confident that their five-star rooms above a quintessential English country pub, The Bear and Swan, in Chew Magna, Somerset, will impress their guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Guesthouse East in Eastbourne, where childhood sweethearts Tony and Lisa hope their guests will fall head over heels for their family friendly B&B.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Shirley B&B in Bournemouth, where Jorge Mesquita hopes to impress his guests with his all-round cleanliness and the power of his showers.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&B owners meet for the last time. The Hillcroft kicks things off and hosts Martyn and Elaine want answers from both Tony and Lisa and Sam and Henry.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: We catch up with two sets of first-time buyers who took on a tough market with Kirstie and Phil's help. Elly and Paul wanted a period home with two bedrooms in Bristol. And Steph and Nick were looking for a futureproof property in central Scotland.

programme.desc: President Obonjo the exiled dictator fights back after an attempted coup by the West. With his army of comedians, he garners support to reinstate himself as the original African dictator.

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Butterfly Effect
programme.desc: A moving, inspirational hour of stigma-busting gags, multimedia projection, hidden camera filming and animation.

programme.title: Kiri Pritchard McLean: Hysterical Woman
programme.desc: Welsh-born comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean asks "Why is 'female' treated as a genre?"

programme.title: Ali Brice: Bin Wondering
programme.desc: A master of the surrealist arts, Ali Brice takes the audience on a journey through his mind at a time when he spent several hours in a wheelie bin.

programme.title: Lou Conran: At Least I'm Not Dog Poo Darren
programme.desc: Lou is vulgar and fearless, demonstrating the best of storytelling comedy; it's about presumption, misunderstandings, misgivings and a bloke called Darren.

programme.title: Anna Mann: How We Stop the Fascists
programme.desc: Anna Mann, aka 'greatest actress of her generation,' and Colin Hoult's alter-ego, takes the audience on a whirlwind journey, to understand where fascism can spring from.

programme.title: Tom Ward: Sex, Snails & Cassette Tapes
programme.desc: An hour of laidback, offbeat observational comedy from comedian Tom Ward.

programme.title: Amy Gledhill: At the Rich Mix
programme.desc: Standup comedian Amy Gledhill performs at Rich Mix in London on 16 October 2020.

programme.title: Peter Brush: Dreams with Advert Breaks
programme.desc: A highly imaginative performer & deft writer of finely crafted jokes, Peter's stage presence perfectly compliments his arsenal of razor sharp wit.

programme.desc: Zoe Zebra delivers a very special letter for Peppa and George: an invitation to her birthday party.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George go ice skating, but they have never been before, and Peppa keeps falling over.

programme.desc: When Peppa and George go to the dentist for a check-up, Dr Elephant says their teeth are nice and white, but George's toy dinosaur is in need of a tooth-clean.

programme.desc: Peppa and family are driving in their large campervan across America to get to Hollywood. They stop at a diner to ask for directions and meet another Miss Rabbit. programme.desc: Peppa looks after Madame Gazelle's guinea pigs Ginger and Brian while she goes on holiday, but prove to be more trouble for Mummy and Daddy Pig than they think.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George and Daddy Pig visit the charity shop to sell things they don't need, and things they may need.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends make up stories about their families. Even Penny gets a picture too.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George, Grandpa and Granny go owl-spotting.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The family visit the apple tree, and notice how it changes every season as they keep visiting it.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George find themselves struggling to walk up the big hill.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Everyone becomes hippies for the day and Grampy Rabbit is the leader.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George check out Grandpa's new Bug Hotel.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The Playgroup play many games using a parachute.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy and Daddy Pig show Peppa and George how to play flying discs in the park. Soon their friends appear with their parents.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mr. Potato visits the playgroup to teach the children a Kiddie Workout with many differnt exercises.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends visit the Little Cars Play Centre where they get to drive little toy cars.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family team up with Danny Dog to swing across the monkey trees.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family hire a canoe from Miss Rabbit. They imagine they are in a jungle and then unexpectedly bump into Mrs Crocodile. Later on, Mr Lion invites them to have a picnic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The adventures of Peppa Pig and friends continue with more fun and games.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family are going to the Water Park. Miss Rabbit is in charge. There are lots of fun things to do at the water park but first you need to put on your swimsuit.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George have come to play at Granny and Grandpa's house. Everyone dresses as dinosaurs having lots of fun and a bit of a party.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Pedro loves superheroes. He has a superhero-themed party and Super Potato is one of the guests.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa Pig and Suzy Sheep are spending the day with Doctor Hamster, and they are learning about what vets do. Doctor Hamster says she's glad to have two helpers with her and calls the next patient.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and Rebecca Rabbit pretend to be babies to get Mummy Rabbit's attention. Mummy Rabbit plays along until they admit they are not really babies!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is on a play date with Candy Cat at Mr Cat's Cafe. Candy show Mr Cat the drawings they've done of everyone, and he hangs them up for everyone to see.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a cold day, Daddy Pig suggests they build a den until Mummy Pig has finished work. After a hard day's work, the cushion den is a perfect place to watch TV together!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are watching TV... until it breaks! While Grampy Rabbit fixes it, the family get a new TV which can do everything... but it doesn't beat their old TV.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A storm cancels Peppa and her family's adventure plans so they decide to take it inside. Using their imagination, they go sledding, climb a mountain and have a campfire in the attic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are playing in their pool. They help the garden animals cool down but when it rains, the animals are happy and so is Peppa. Rain makes muddy puddles for jumping in!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George are in Granny and Grandpa Pig's Garden. Granny's friends are playing folk music! They play music for Grandpa Pig and then Grandpa Pig makes apple juice for everyone!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is bedtime and Peppa calls Kylie Kangaroo and is surprised to learn that it is not morning or winter where she is! Daddy Pig explains this using a big ball to represent the Earth.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While visiting Suzy and Emily's house, Peppa is surprised that there are extra rules which she does not have in her own house. On the drive home, Peppa explains to Mummy Pig that different houses have different rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig is confirming she is having a new baby. Mummy and Daddy Pig share the exciting news with Peppa and her brother, George.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family are on the search for a new home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and family renovate their home.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: With the building work complete, it's time to decorate! Peppa and George have fun choosing how to decorate their new attic bedrooms. It's dinosaurs for George, but what will Peppa pick?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa's family car breaks down. While it is being repaired, they borrow a shiny new one from Grandad Dog's garage.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig are taking Peppa and George to a festival - a jigsaw festival! Peppa is amazed by all of the completed jigsaws around the festival.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle is making a wicker basket. She explains it is her hobby - something you do to relax. The children discuss hobbies and learn about each other's favourite things to do.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig and Peppa are at Granny Pig's house. They find Mummy's old patchwork quilt that Granny made for her as a baby. Peppa decides she'd like to make one for the baby!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is out with Suzy and Granny Sheep in Mummy Sheep's car. They come across an old van that Miss Rabbit is recycling. Granny Sheep decides she would like to turn it into an art van.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny Sheep is driving Vanessa, the art van along the road with Peppa, Suzy, Pedro, Mandy, Molly and Danny. They are taking her to Grampy Rabbit's boat yard, for her first-ever outing!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: While Mummy and Daddy Pig attend a refresher antenatal class, Peppa and George join Miss Rabbit's Sibling School where they learn about what to expect when their new sibling arrives.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is so excited to meet her new baby brother or sister, but they are taking forever to arrive until suddenly Mummy Pig announces the baby is coming, now!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is determined to find the perfect name for the baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The nurse is popping around, and she decides that today's visit should be all about how to hold a baby. The nurse shows Peppa a special way for a small person to safely hold a baby.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George play at Granny and Grandpa Pig's house. Grandma, Peppa and George go to the shed to see what Grandpa is doing and discover that he's built a robot.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends learn how to animate their drawings at playgroup.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mom, Dad, Peppa and George decide to go to the beach on a beautiful sunny day. Suddenly, a little cloud covers the sun for a minute, but then it passes and the sun reappears.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.sub-title: Mr Bull Digs Up the River

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a rainy day, Granny Sheep plays in the garden with Peppa and Suzy. They decide to play with cardboard boxes and build a hospital, a cookie factory, and a train station.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The children want to make their own takeaway. Together they 'cook' and deliver the pretend takeaways to the parents. They get great reviews, but nothing beats a real lunch!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George play with walkie-talkies at Granny and Grandpa Pig's house. Grandpa Pig teaches Peppa and George how to use codenames to talk using the gadgets.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: At the playground, Pedro and Penny's glasses fall off; both think that their glasses are broken, as they cannot see very well; Madame Gazelle shows Pedro and Penny different types of objects with lenses.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It's a snowy day and Peppa's family build an igloo for their snowman. They hide in it during a snowstorm, but Penny Polar Bear and her parents love snowstorms - and also Peppa's igloo!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are at playgroup when they learn that Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow are getting married the next day; everyone goes into a panic when they confess how they have done nothing to prepare for the big day.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Everyone prepares for Mr. Bull and Mrs. Cow's wedding; Peppa and the soon-to-be-weds go to the jewellers to get rings, to the dress makers, to get a wedding dress and finally to Peppa's house to sort the wedding cake.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The day of Mrs. Cow and Mr. Bull wedding arrives, but not everything goes to plan; nothing stops it being the best wedding ever and there is a lot of happy crying.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family go bowling at the Peppatown Bowling Alley.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit is teaching the children a swimming lesson, They start out by swimming with floats tucked under their arms.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa Pig discovers what a cruise ship is, and goes on a cruise to a country called Greece.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: There are lots of activities to do on the cruise, like breakfast at the pirate cafe. There, they meet Pirate Pete and Mrs Mermaid who invite everyone to their show.

programme.desc: Peppa and George go sailing with Grandpa Pig, but when he keeps getting stuck in the mud, they all decide to have a sleepover on the boat while they wait for the tide to come back in.

programme.desc: There is a new girl at school, and she quickly makes best friends with Peppa and Rebecca Rabbit. Molly shows them how good she is at digging while taking them on a tour of her underground house.

programme.desc: After learning about a bunch of eccentric holidays like Rock Day, Paper vows to get a holiday of his own; Scissors goes deep into his mind to decide on whether to help his roommates bring in the groceries.

programme.title: Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: The Turtles and Mutanimals are caught in the storm of the century; when a new group of sea mutants threaten the city, Raph will be forced to use his words to save the day.

programme.title: Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: Mikey and Genghis Frog are sent into the storm on a supply run to the local market when they encounter the electrifying Lee The Eeel; now Mikey has to step up and be a leader.

programme.title: Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.sub-title: Splinter and April Fight a Goldfish
programme.desc: When the Lair floods, April goes to save Splinter, but he won't leave the sewer; to make matters worse, the mutant called Goldfin comes with a warning stay out of his way, or else.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore is passed over when Alvin and Eleanor train as lifeguards; Miss Croner's cherries are lost.

programme.title: Rock Paper Scissors
programme.desc: When Scissors neglects to take out the trash, a medieval kingdom of living rubbish takes over the apartment; Paper tries to make Scissors do his one chore while Rock looks for the perfect fantasy romance.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: The Orchid Grief; Forks and Knives Out
programme.desc: When Lincoln and Clyde overwater Howard's rare orchid, the two embark on an adventure to replace it; Lynn Sr. is stoked to compete on a televised cooking show until he learns he's up against Rosa Casagrande.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: The Loud Cloud; You Auto Know Better
programme.desc: Tired of Mom's sticky note system, Lincoln and Lisa create an app to keep track of the family's schedule; Lana creates an auto body shop to try and buy a new bike, but her shop turns corrupt when Flip becomes her mentor.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Bubble Trouble; The Way of the Sponge
programme.desc: When SpongeBob and Patrick accidentally destroy Sandy's air supply in 'Bubble Trouble' and then in 'The Way Of The Sponge', SpongeBob wants to earn his belt from karate master Fuzzy Acorns.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Annoyed by SpongeBob for the last time, Squidward goes legal in `Restraining SpongeBob', and in `Fiasco!', Plankton steals a Krabby Patty, unaware it's really an art piece by famous artist Fiasco.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Last Days Of Summer / The Littlest Robot
programme.desc: Alvin wastes his entire summer playing a video game then tries to do all the fun things he'd planned in one day; Simon invents a robot with emotions and an ability to learn.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: You Troll; Life of the Party
programme.desc: The Chipmunks try to keep Dave from getting on the Internet after a performance he posts gets negative comments. The Chipmunks along with Kevin and Cheesy decide to crash BoCarter's birthday yacht party after not being invited.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Mr Krabs resorts to desperate measures after Plankton drives away his customers in `Free Samples', and then in `Home Sweet Rubble', SpongeBob's pineapple is falling apart, and needs re-modelling.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton's computer wife Karen ends up working at the Krusty Krab after being replaced by a newer model in 'Karen 2.0', and in 'InSPONGEiac', SpongeBob can't get to sleep, and asks Patrick to help.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Lisa gets invited to work at an institute and get away from her crazy family. Lori and Bobby worry that dating long distance is causing them to lose their connection.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Lincoln and Clyde worry that they may be too old to still be playing Ace Savvy. Lincoln discovers that having a friend over means getting special privileges and he takes advantage of that.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.sub-title: Oh Brother Where Art Thou; The Wall
programme.desc: Alvin thinks he's losing his mind when Simon goes missing and no one else even remembers who Simon is. When the girls at school cast votes for the cutest chipmunk on the bathroom wall, Alvin worries about the results.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Theodore gives Dave an ugly handmade vest for his birthday and insists he wear it to an upcoming business meeting. When Theodore finds a winning lottery ticket, everyone has their own ideas on how the money should be spent.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: The Two Faces of Squidward; Spongehenge
programme.desc: SpongeBob accidentally messes up Squidward's face in `The Two Faces of Squidward', and then in `Spongehenge', SpongeBob's sponge brings all the jellyfish to the city.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Banned in Bikini Bottom; Stanley S. SquarePants
programme.desc: Two episodes of the cartoon about an accident-prone sea sponge who works in a fast-food restaurant and has adventures with his friends Patrick Starfish, Squidward and others.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Zach and Rusty fake an alien landing that sends the whole town on a hunt for little green men; Clyde thinks he's guaranteed the Glee Club solo, until Lincoln accidentally auditions and lands the part.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.sub-title: Hurl, Interrupted; Diamonds Are For Never
programme.desc: Lynn tries to stall her friends from riding the coaster; Lola and Mr. Grouse find a gem.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: When Simon falls ill, Alvin takes his place on a camping trip only to discover it's actually an archaeological dig; the Chipmunks don't know what to do when they suspect Dave is lying about the various awards he's won.

programme.title: Alvinnn!!! and the Chipmunks
programme.desc: Brittany hastily enters a skateboard contest against a skate park bully even though she has no clue how to ride; complaints rise as Simon's robot Geizmo goes around town dismantling washing machines.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Rise and Shine & Waiting; Fungus Among Us
programme.desc: We check on Patrick's morning routine in `Rise and Shine', SpongeBob must be patient for his cereal-based entertainment in `Waiting', and then in `Fungus Among Us', SpongeBob develops a case of Ick!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: Spy Buddies; Boat Smarts; Good Ol' Whathisname
programme.desc: Three episodes: `Spy buddies, Boat smarts and Good Ol' what's his name?'.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.sub-title: New Digs; Krabs a la Mode
programme.desc: Two episodes: `New digs' and `Krabs à la mode'.

programme.title: The Loud House
programme.desc: Hoping to get closer to her crush, Benny, Luan tries out for the school play. When Clyde and schoolmate Zach begin bonding over a mutual interest in antiques, Lincoln quickly finds himself feeling like a third wheel.

programme.title: Boogie Nights!

programme.title: The World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver continues his journey through the world of Ancient Britain as he encounters an age of cosmological priests and some of the greatest monuments of the Stone Age including Stonehenge itself. This is a time of elite travellers who were inventing the very idea of Heaven itself.

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: After the Charles II's brother, the Catholic James VII & II, was deposed by protestant William of Orange in 1688, Britain became a constitutional monarchy. But the so-called 1688 `Glorious Revolution' came at a price.

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory

programme.title: Impossible Peace
programme.desc: Americans continue to have a good time despite the Eighteenth Amendment banning alcohol, and a mood of reckless optimism throughout the country drives investment in the stock market.

programme.title: Elton John - Live in London 2013
programme.desc: Filmed at the Roundhouse in London, this concert film gives fans a live experience from start to finish; Elton John performs his hits: "Tiny Dancer," "Rocket Man," "Your Song," and many more.

programme.title: Imagine Dragons Live at the Moody Theater
programme.desc: Pop rock group Imagine Dragons plays to a crowd in Austin, Texas, at SXSW on Mar. 11, 2014; songs include "Fallen," "Hear Me," "It's Time," "Demons," "Radioactive" and "Nothing Left to Say."

programme.title: John Legend - Live at the iTunes Festival
programme.desc: John Legend is an artist with intellectual skills, he studied English with a major in African American literature at the University of Pennsylvania. In college, he participated in a jazz group called Counterparts and at the time was introduced to singer Lauryn Hill, playing piano on the track 'Everything Is Everything' from the singer's first solo album.

programme.title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
programme.desc: Celebrating the 50th anniversary of their landmark album, Days of Future Passed, The Moody Blues took to the road to perform the entire LP live. An electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance of this seminal album.

programme.title: Oasis: Live at Wembley Arena 2008
programme.desc: Filmed at Wembley Arena, London, this concert film gives all fans a spectacular live experience from start to finish.

programme.title: Paul McCartney: The Space Within Us
programme.desc: Paul McCartney performs Beatles hits and other songs in concert.

programme.title: Eric Clapton & Steve Winwood - Live from Madison Square Garden
programme.desc: Eric Clapton and Steve Winwood perform at Madison Square Garden.

programme.title: Paul Rodgers: Free Spirit
programme.desc: The 16-song collection, 'Free Spirit', filmed in London, England returned Paul Rodgers to his roots, bringing fans an extraordinary evening.

programme.title: Interpol - Rock En Seine
programme.desc: Interpol is an American rock band from Manhattan, New York, [2] formed in 1997. Their original line-up consisted of Paul Banks, Daniel Kessler, Carlos Dengler and Greg Drudy.

programme.title: Linkin Park - Live in New York
programme.desc: Filmed at the Madison Square Garden in New York, this concert film gives all fans a spectacular live experience from start to finish; Linkin Park performs hits including "Numb," "In the End," "Bleed It Out" and many more.

programme.title: Public Enemy: Live at The Metro Theatre
programme.desc: Public Enemy, also known as PE, is an influential hip hop group from Long Island, New York, known for its politically charged lyrics, criticism of the media, and active interest in the concerns of the African American community. In 2004, Rolling Stone Magazine ranked Public Enemy number forty-four on its list of the Immortals: 100. Here, the group performs at The Metro Theatre, in Sydney, Australia, on December 27, 2008.

programme.title: Guns N' Roses: Live From The O2 Arena London
programme.desc: Watching this mega-production of Guns N' Roses performing live at the O2 Arena to its fans singing and vibrating to each song including Paradise City, Sweet Child O' Mine, November Rain and many more.

programme.title: Charli XCX: Reading & Leeds Festival 2022
programme.desc: Charli XCX performs live at the Reading and Leeds Festivals in England.

programme.title: George Michael -- Live in London
programme.desc: The singer performs at Earls Court Exhibition Centre in London; songs include "Father Figure," "Faith," "Amazing" and "Freedom 90."

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.sub-title: A Little Lady Makes Big Money
programme.desc: Angus finds a rare porcelain figure who's worth big bucks. A widow gets a surprise when her late husband's collection sells for a small fortune.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First, art conservator Lucia Scalisi faces the thrilling task of restoring a rare 17th-century painting of Queen Henrietta Maria, the wife of Charles I. For owner Howard Bird, the painting is invaluable as it represents a lifeline between himself and a dear friend who sadly passed away. As Lucia scales away the layers of dark overpaint, she makes a discovery.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. A daunting task has ceramics restorer Kirsten Ramsay worried. Jayne Marston has brought in her parent's four-foot-high, heavy stone bird bath in the hope Kirsten can preserve it. For over 60 years, wherever Jayne's family moved the bird bath would follow, uprooted and replaced at the new house with great effort by her dad. Jayne has lost both her parents over the last three years and during a long illness her mum would take comfort watching the birds at the bath. Jayne's mum made her promise to keep feeding the birds and to keep water in the bath, telling Jayne 'whenever the birds are near so am I'. However, the bird bath is now showing its age, and with a huge crack threatening to break it in two, Kirsten's restoration skills will be pushed to their limit.

For over 60 years, wherever Jayne's family moved the bird bath would follow, uprooted and replaced at the new house with great effort by her dad. Jayne has lost both her parents over the last three years and during a long illness her mum would take comfort watching the birds at the bath. Jayne's mum made her promise to keep feeding the birds and to keep water in the bath, telling Jayne 'whenever the birds are near so am I'. However, the bird bath is now showing its age, and with a huge crack threatening to break it in two, Kirsten's restoration skills will be pushed to their limit.

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: American Detective With Lt. Joe Kenda
programme.desc: A young mother is viciously gunned down on Virginia's Chincoteague Island.

programme.desc: Humberside Police investigate the identity of a man whose body is found in a park in Hull, and what emerges is a shocking cover-up by a mother and her son. programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2017, a neighbour finds the body of 40-year-old Dawn Meade outside an apartment complex in Louisa, Va.; detectives must determine if this is an accidental death or something more sinister, so they turn to surveillance footage to uncover the truth.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Forensic Detectives

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Joann Albanese finds love again with a personal trainer named John Edwards, then John moves in but Joann discovers too late her new boyfriend isn't who he claims to be.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Ashley Pittman thinks she hit the lottery marrying Jeffrey Scott, and he gives her a cushier life than the one she left behind in small-town Louisiana; however, behind closed doors, a secret soon turns deadly.

programme.title: American Detective With Lt. Joe Kenda
programme.desc: The former mayor of Potowan Point, Ohio, turns up dead in his hunting cabin. Detective Ryan Allar and his team begin the search to figure out if this is political retaliation or something more personal, what they find hits a little too close to home.

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: When 15-year-old Danielle Jones went missing on her way to school in 2001, it turned into one of the most high-profile cases in 20 years. Essex Police give an insight into the investigation that convicted her uncle, Stuart Campbell, of her murder.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In September 2014, an Iowa college town panics after police find Tong Shao, a missing student from China, murdered; when security camera footage points to a planned execution, hunting down the young woman's killer becomes an international mystery.

programme.title: People Magazine Presents: Crimes of the 2000s
programme.desc: When an exotic animal keeper vanishes on her way to work at San Diego's Wild Animal Park in the fall of 2000, investigators unearth several suspects, but only one who had ample opportunity to go in for the kill.

programme.title: People Magazine Presents: Crimes of the 2000s
programme.desc: In 2001, a massive explosion decimates a home on a quiet Scottsdale, Ariz., street; the bodies of three family members lie amid the rubble, but one key person is missing, and the search goes on for decades.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When a man disappears in Hagerstown, Maryland, the only clue to his whereabouts is the white BMW he abandons at an apartment complex. Investigators turn to CCTV footage to piece together his final movements and unravel a deadly plot.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the brutal slaying of a homeless man, James Tony Wise, Portland PD, turns to the footage from CCTV cameras to find his killer; detectives use the images to retrace the rampage through the city of a violent offender.

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: Sometimes blood relations lead to bloodshed: when murder becomes a family affair, investigators must turn to forensics to uncover family plots.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Germany's Mystic Forest
programme.desc: 2.000 rivers and streams dig deep into the underground and transport their water into reservoirs or Germany's Wild Amazon, the Wupper. Martens, badgers, wild boars, hares, roe deer, and red deer, even black grouse and hazel deer find shelter in these parts. Wolves also have a dominant presence, not to mention those who live underground, such as lizards, bats, and snakes.

programme.title: American Night
programme.desc: An art dealer and a ruthless New York mafia boss fight for money, power and love.

programme.title: Bloodshot
programme.desc: After he and his wife are murdered, marine Ray Garrison is resurrected by a team of scientists. Enhanced with nanotechnology, he becomes a superhuman, biotech killing machine - Bloodshot. As Ray first trains with fellow super-soldiers, he cannot recall anything from his former life. But when his memories flood back and he remembers the man that killed both him and his wife, he breaks out of the facility to get revenge, only to discover that there's more to the conspiracy than he thought.

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire

programme.title: Demolition Man
programme.desc: Violence has been outlawed in the politically correct regime of 2032, and the only weapons in existence are consigned to a museum, but when an escaped psychopath heads straight for the museum, the authorities decide the only effective recourse they have is to thaw out a violent, cryogenically frozen cop.

programme.title: Wonder Woman
programme.desc: Diana, princess of the Amazons, is raised to be an unconquerable warrior on a sheltered island paradise. She discovers her full powers and true destiny after leaving home to join the fight to end all wars.

programme.title: Uncharted

programme.title: Aquaman
programme.desc: Aquaman must retrieve the legendary Trident of Atlan to save the underwater city of Atlantis -- and the surface world -- from his power-hungry brother.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Bad Boys II
programme.desc: Loose-cannon cops Lowrey and Burnett reduce Miami to rubble as they try to stop Cuban and Russian drug smugglers from flooding Florida with ecstasy.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a small frontier town in the American southwest, provoked into a gunfight in which he kills three men, and then begged by the townsfolk to become sheriff. The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.title: Mamma Mia!
programme.desc: Set on a Greek island, a young woman who is about to be married suspects that any one of three men could be her father. She invites them all to her wedding without telling her mother. Movie adaptation of the musical, set to Abba's greatest hits.

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Jack Malik is a struggling singer-songwriter in an English seaside town whose dreams of fame are rapidly fading, despite the fierce devotion and support of his childhood best friend, Ellie. After a freak bus accident during a mysterious global blackout, Jack wakes up to discover that The Beatles have never existed. Performing songs by the greatest band in history to a world that has never heard them, Jack becomes on overnight sensation with a little help from his agent.

programme.desc: Harry has been partying for 48 hours when he meets Johannes on the dance floor of a club in Berlin. With 15 hours until his flight home, Johannes offers to help him print his boarding pass.

programme.title: ...And Justice for All
programme.desc: Satirical drama in which an idealistic lawyer who tries to win cases for clients trapped in a hypocritical and corrupt legal system finds himself defending a venal and self-righteous judge accused of rape. The judge wrongly assumes the lawyer's ideals and disdain for the courts will drive him to get an acquittal, despite the evidence and the loathing they have for each other.

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby and his dying mother. Her dreary life is interrupted when her schoolgirl crush arrives back in town.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Jerry Maguire
programme.desc: A sports agent who makes the mistake of saying what he believes, finds himself sacked and abandoned by all his clients apart from small time footballer Rod Tidwell. Maguire's personal life starts to fall apart as he's torn between love and money in Cameron Crowe's metaphor on Americans' need for success and money.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Dirty Harry
programme.desc: A violent and uncompromising police officer hunts for a psychopath who holds the city of San Francisco to ransom. Although the officer's unorthodox methods do not endear him to his superiors, the mayor reluctantly agrees that Harry Callahan seems to be the right cop for the job.

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Dr Richard Kemble's wife is murdered and he is arrested for the crime. He soon realises he has been framed, and while being transported on a prison bus, he manages to escape. On the run, he has to evade the relentless pursuit by the US Marshall, Samuel Gerard, while also avoiding everyone he knows, and finding out who the real killer is, the one-armed man who broke into their house.

programme.title: The Firm
programme.desc: A young lawyer joins a small but prestigious law firm only to find out that most of their clients are on the wrong side of the law. The company is helping to launder mob money, get clients off charges and even murder partners who threaten to blow their cover, but when the FBI come calling to gather evidence on the lawyer's colleagues, he is caught between a rock and a hard place, juggling his life and his liberty.

programme.title: Amadeus
programme.desc: Once the highly regarded composer to the Viennese Court, Salieri is now an old man, unknown and confined to an asylum. After a suicide attempt he recounts his story to a young cleric, going back 30 years to his heyday at the court of Emperor Joseph II, where he first met the young Mozart. Despite Salieri's Machiavellian attempts to frustrate Mozart's progress, the great young composer attracts the favour of the Emperor.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: The Namaqua Rock Fig Tree stands rooted on the slope of a rocky hill in Namaqualand, South Africa. It is the ultimate survivor, able to withstand the unforgiving conditions in a landscape facing ever rising temperatures and less and less rainfall each year.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: The Eastern Cape Province is investing heavily in ecotourism and conservation as an alternative to fishing and commercial development. The region is reclaiming farmlands and closing fishing grounds for wildlife. This is one of the most exciting conservation success stories in Africa.

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Track scientists who have arrived in the Kalahari to test a hypothesis: that animal collaboration is the key to the dizzying diversity of the region's species. Observe as they monitor behaviour, decipher data, and deliver fascinating conclusions.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: In India, Ravi the baby lion-tailed macaque faces life in a shrinking habitat and encroaching human world. Luckily he has a caring mum, a protective dad, a playful sister, and numerous, cousins, aunts and uncles all watching over him.

programme.desc: Patrick is in California as he searches for answers to why underwater are forests in sharp decline, and why otters might be the solution.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Three locations, one investigation. The Heritage Centre, Lafferty's Pub, and The Bell Inn give Derby a claim on the title of 'the most haunted city in Europe'. programme.title: Great Raids of World War II
programme.desc: As soon as RAF Bomber Command struck back at Nazi Germany, it began to suffer unsustainable losses. A team of British paratroopers was sent to Bruneval to seize a radar system and its precious wavelengths in order to drastically reduce the losses suffered. This documentary explores the raid with the use of veterans' accounts, archive film, and reconstructions.

programme.title: WWII POW Escape from Wales
programme.desc: In 1945, 70 German POWs escaped from the high security camp in Bridgend in Wales, and were tracked by armed soldiers, Home Guard, dogs and children, resulting in one of the largest manhunts of WWII.

Later, the Stig races around the track against an aerobatic plane, the lads find out how much faster a car can go when it is stripped of all its non-essential equipment, and Gordon Ramsay is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: In the season one closer, the guys give awards for Car of the Year, Most Pointless Car, and Weirdest Renault; among others. Reviews are given of the Toyota Land Cruiser, BMW X5, Jeep Grand Cherokee, and Range Rover. Clarkson fondly remembers the Lotus Esprit, Dawe looks at a number of car bargains, and there is a second attempt to find the `Fastest Faith'.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si explore the history behind our greatest staple food - the potato. From a warming Lancashire potato and butter pie to a chicken casserole with potato cobbler, recipes that show the unassuming tuber at its best. They also look at how British potato vodka is winning global awards and remember a dark time in Welsh history when the fish and chip shops almost ran out of chips.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins his journey in the south of Ireland, para-motoring with an Irish explorer. On the west coast he does spot of surfing, before climbing Croagh Patrick in honour of Ireland's patron saint. This leg of his journey ends in Malin Head, Ireland's most northerly point.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard continues on his quest to destroy as many caravans as possible, and James May makes his debut. Tough-man Vinnie Jones is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May look at luxurious motoring as Jeremy checks out the Rolls Royce Phantom, and Richard revisits the finest in 1960s style and comfort. Don't forget UKTV People's special event - Car of the Year 2004. Just look out for the nominated cars and interactive voting instructions in between each episode of Top Gear.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si enjoy the flavours of home as they explore Britain's love of comfort food. As well as a classic rice pudding, the Bikers prepare their own tastes of home, with Dave's stuffed cabbage leaves and Si's flat rib broth. They also reveal the history of the school dinner and see what comfort food means to all Britons, from the Notting Hill Carnival to Shabbat dinner.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: After Hitler's initial territorial gains in Russia, the relentless advance of the Wehrmacht is decisively stopped at Stalingrad. For the first time in the war, the Nazis suffer heavy losses in what will later be regarded as one of the decisive turning points of the conflict.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The German attempts to starve Britain by attacking ships bringing supplies from North America are very nearly successful. Many tonnes of shipping are sunk and many lives are lost to U-Boat attacks. Defeat in the Atlantic would surely mean the end of the war for the Allies.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: A documentary series that gives a historical account of the events of World War II, from its roots in the 1920s to the aftermath and the lives it profoundly influenced.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: While Bomber Command are unable to hit their targets precisely and begin bombing German cities under cover of darkness, the Americans reinforce the daytime attacks.

programme.title: Black Ops
programme.desc: Dom and Kay are two ordinary police community support officers on the front line of policing, handing out frisbees with 'Stay Street Smart' printed on them in east London. Detective Inspector Clinton Blair needs two undercover officers to infiltrate the Brightmarsh Gang, but the Met chiefs don't seem to get the brief, so Clinton decides to take matters into his own hands and recruits Dom and Kay in an 'off-the-books' operation.

programme.desc: Finding themselves unexpectedly in a country ditch, Foggy, Clegg and Compo decide that one standard gent's bicycle doesn't really meet their combined transportation needs. programme.title: Black Ops
programme.desc: Dom and Kay are two ordinary police community support officers on the front line of policing, handing out frisbees with 'Stay Street Smart' printed on them in east London. Detective Inspector Clinton Blair needs two undercover officers to infiltrate the Brightmarsh Gang, but the Met chiefs don't seem to get the brief, so Clinton decides to take matters into his own hands and recruits Dom and Kay in an 'off-the-books' operation.

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Spin-off from `Only Fools and Horses', featuring the characters of Boycie and Marlene adapting to life in rural Shropshire. When Bryan and Elgin suffer a housing crisis, Boycie and Marlene offer them sanctuary, but Boycie's benevolence towards his workers brings an unexpected reward of immortality.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: When the standard of canteen food goes from bad to worse, the staff decide to run the canteen themselves.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A chance encounter with an unwilling and resentful dog-walker suggests to Foggy that this might be the business opportunity he has been waiting for all his life. With the reluctant assistance of Compo and Clegg, he sets out to prove that `walkies' could mean big money for them.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Finding themselves unexpectedly in a country ditch, Foggy, Clegg and Compo decide that one standard gent's bicycle doesn't really meet their combined transportation needs. programme.title: Rise of the Nazis

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return with a second helping from a recent visit to Hertford College, Oxford. Objects under the cameras include a bracelet once worn by a princess with a tragic story; expensive ceramics bought for a song at a boot sale; and a rare document recording the end of hostilities in the First World War. Plus, one of the experts shows his hand as an accomplished amateur potter.

programme.desc: A huge turnout awaits Fiona Bruce and the team at Lincoln Cathedral. programme.title: Hello Charlie

programme.title: 420 IPC
